title: 'YES! Come play!' — group welcome (Nano Banana group-shot locked, v2)
preRenderNote: Composition is anchored by the Nano Banana 3-character group still (group_ep13_clip16_welcome.png). Sara (pink helmet, arms wide welcoming), Eva (teal helmet, hands cupped at mouth), Isabel (no helmet, hands clasped at chest, distinctively brunette curly hair). Locks the count at exactly 3 + visual differentiators (helmets vs no helmet, blonde vs brunette).
prompt: Wide shot of @tennis-court — green court with white painted lines, chain-link fence softly visible BACKGROUND, soft warm afternoon light. The composition is anchored by the @ep13-clip16-group pre-rendered group still — exactly THREE girls locked in place: Sara CENTER-LEFT (pink helmet, arms welcoming), Eva CENTER-RIGHT (teal helmet, hands cupped at mouth), Isabel FAR-RIGHT (no helmet, brunette curls, hands clasped at chest). Kling animates the still gently — smiles widen, gentle in-place sway, hands move softly within their locked poses. POSITION LOCK — every girl stays in her EXACT same physical spot from the source still throughout the entire 10s. Exactly THREE people in the entire frame. Distinct visual differentiators (pink helmet / teal helmet / no helmet, blonde / blonde-curly / brunette-curly) prevent sister mix-up. (BEAT 1 — 0-5s) Sara waves both her open hands warmly in welcome from her locked spot. Sara (warm cheerful kid-voice, sing-song bright): "YES! Come play with us!" Eva, hands still cupped at her mouth from her locked spot, calls out cheerfully. Eva (cheerful sing-song call): "We have a tennis ball! Catch it with us!" (BEAT 2 — 5-10s) Isabel beams brightly from her locked spot, both hands still clasped at her chest. Isabel (kind kindergartener voice, warm and shy with delight): "Yay! Thank you! What do we play first?" Sara (cheerful kid-voice, both hands wide in locked pose): "TAG! Everyone's IT!" Calm cheerful warm kid-show family ambiance throughout. STRICT ANATOMY: each girl has exactly two arms and two hands at all times. Each girl stays grounded with both feet on the green court throughout — no leaping, no jumping, no running. Helmets stay buckled.
